reasoning: The PKGBUILD follows standard AUR binary package patterns, fetching from official GitHub releases over HTTPS. While skipped checksums are suboptimal, they're an accepted AUR practice for binary packages and don't constitute active malicious behavior. No code execution risks were found in package functions.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen incorrectly treated checksum skipping as an active security risk rather than a trust/convenience trade-off common in AUR binaries
false_positives: Qwen's 'Unverified binary download' - SKIP checksums are permitted for AUR binaries and don't automatically make the package unsafe
learnings: Improve understanding of AUR-specific practices around checksum skipping for binary packages
Prioritize PKGBUILD analysis over non-executed files
Differentiate between security risks vs. package quality issues in assessments
